specific_location: First Creek Park, Green Valley Ranch
description: The first bird we encountered when entering First Creek Park from the north, moving only a few feet from the ground in a cottonwood, where photographs were taken. We lost it higher in the same tree but then repeatedly encountered it at slightly greater distance at all heights in additional cottonwoods as it slowly worked eastward along the creek.See photos. Seems like it would need to be a very bright and clean Cassin’s otherwise. Dark blue-gray head (evoking more of a Plumbeous than the average Cassin’s) contrasting well with greenish back, clean white throat and spectacles sharply contrasting with head plumage with a well defined margin, not diffuse at the edges. Bright yellow flanks and vent strap, contrasting with clean white below.
